description: Automated browser agent that scrolls social media feeds and collects content via Playwright API interception. Part of the Focus Lab vision for replacing social media scrolling with AI-curated, goal-aligned content.

userStories.title: Project Scaffolding
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the project structure set up with all necessary files so that I can start building features immediately.
acceptanceCriteria: src/ directory exists with __init__.py, collector.py, auth.py, interceptor.py, scroller.py, media_downloader.py, storage.py, models.py
requirements.txt exists with playwright as dependency
config.json exists with default settings (scroll_delay_min: 2, scroll_delay_max: 5, max_tweets: 50, output_dir: feed_data)
.gitignore excludes session/, feed_data/, __pycache__/, .venv/, *.pyc
python src/collector.py runs without errors

userStories.title: Session Management
userStories.description: As a user, I want to log into Twitter once and have my session saved so that subsequent collection runs don't require manual login.
acceptanceCriteria: Running python src/auth.py opens a Chromium browser to twitter.com for manual login
After login, browser state is saved to session/twitter_state.json
load_session() launches browser with saved state and verifies login succeeded
If session is missing or expired, a clear error message is shown with instructions to re-authenticate
session/ directory is gitignored

userStories.title: GraphQL Response Interception
userStories.description: As a collector, I want to intercept Twitter's GraphQL API responses when the home feed loads so that I get structured tweet data without DOM scraping.
acceptanceCriteria: page.on('response') listener intercepts responses matching URL pattern */i/api/graphql/*/Home*
Raw JSON response bodies are captured from HomeTimeline and/or HomeLatestTimeline endpoints
Each intercepted response is logged with: endpoint name, HTTP status, response size, number of tweet entries found
Raw responses are saved to feed_data/YYYY-MM-DD/raw/ for debugging
Interception works with a saved session (no manual login needed during collection)

userStories.title: Tweet Data Parsing
userStories.description: As a collector, I want intercepted GraphQL responses parsed into structured tweet objects so that downstream systems have clean, usable data.
acceptanceCriteria: Tweet dataclass defined with fields: id, text, author_handle, author_name, created_at, likes, retweets, replies, quotes, media_urls, is_retweet, original_author
Parser navigates Twitter's nested JSON structure to extract tweet data from timeline entries
Retweets are identified and original author is captured
Promoted/ad tweets are flagged or skipped
Tweets with missing fields (e.g., media-only tweets) are handled gracefully without crashes
At least 10 tweets are successfully parsed from a single interception

userStories.title: Structured Data Output
userStories.description: As a collector, I want parsed tweets saved as structured JSON files so that data persists between runs and is ready for the curation layer.
acceptanceCriteria: Parsed tweets are saved to feed_data/YYYY-MM-DD/tweets.json
JSON is pretty-printed and human-readable
Output includes collection metadata: run timestamp, tweet count, collection duration
Running the collector end-to-end (load session -> navigate -> intercept -> parse -> save) produces a valid tweets.json file
tweets.json can be loaded back into Tweet dataclass objects without errors

userStories.title: Scroll Automation
userStories.description: As a collector, I want the browser to automatically scroll the Twitter feed so that more tweets are loaded and captured beyond the initial page.
acceptanceCriteria: scroll_feed() scrolls the page down and waits a random delay between config scroll_delay_min and scroll_delay_max
Scrolling triggers new GraphQL responses which are intercepted and parsed
Scroll loop detects when no new tweets are being loaded (stale detection after N scrolls with no new tweets)
Scroll behavior is logged: scroll count, new tweets per scroll, total tweets so far

userStories.title: Configurable Stop Conditions
userStories.description: As a user, I want to configure when the collector stops so that I can control collection depth and duration.
acceptanceCriteria: Stop conditions are configurable in config.json: max_tweets, max_minutes, oldest_tweet_date
Collection stops when ANY stop condition is met (whichever comes first)
The reason for stopping is logged clearly
Default: collect up to 50 tweets or 5 minutes, whichever comes first

userStories.title: Tweet Deduplication
userStories.description: As a collector, I want duplicate tweets skipped so that repeated runs don't create redundant data.
acceptanceCriteria: Tweets already present in the current day's tweets.json are not added again
Deduplication is by tweet ID
Number of duplicates skipped is tracked and reported
Within a single run, the same tweet appearing in multiple GraphQL responses is only stored once

userStories.title: Image Download
userStories.description: As a collector, I want images from tweets downloaded locally so that the full content is preserved offline.
acceptanceCriteria: Images are downloaded from media_url_https URLs with ?format=jpg&name=large suffix
Images are saved to feed_data/YYYY-MM-DD/media/{tweet_id}_{index}.jpg
Tweet JSON is updated with local_media_paths field pointing to downloaded files
Failed downloads are logged but do not crash the collector
Download progress is shown (X of Y images downloaded)

userStories.title: Collection Run Summary
userStories.description: As a user, I want a summary printed after each collection run so that I know what was captured.
acceptanceCriteria: After collection completes, a summary is printed with: total tweets captured, new tweets (non-duplicate), images downloaded, total run time, reason for stopping
Summary is also appended to feed_data/YYYY-MM-DD/run_log.json
Summary includes any errors or warnings encountered during the run
